channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Tensions flare in House over Al Green censure
item.description: Tensions flared in the House on Thursday as lawmakers voted to censure Rep. Al Green (D-Texas) for his disruptive protest during President Trump’s address to Congress, prompting a dramatic scene on the House floor — and demands from Republicans for further retribution.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Federal judge reinstates labor board member fired by President Donald Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge ruled Thursday that President Donald Trump acted illegally when he fired a member of an independent labor agency, and the judge ordered that she be allowed to remain on the job. U.S. District Judge Beryl Howell in Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump cuts off federal resources for law firm that helped fuel 2016 Russia hoax
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order Thursday to rescind security clearances and access to federal resources for Perkins Coie, the law firm behind the "Steele dossier.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FDA nominee sidesteps questions on abortion pill, agency layoffs and other issues
item.description: President Donald Trump’s pick to lead the Food and Drug Administration largely sidestepped questions Thursday from senators about how he would handle numerous pressing issues before the agency, including recent layoffs, a canceled vaccine meeting and the continued availability of the abortion pill.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW PERRONE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The blame game around Minnesota’s dimmer budget forecast
item.description: Minnesota House Speaker Lisa Demuth, R-Cold Spring, joined Morning Edition host Cathy Wurzer ahead of the budget forecast release to talk about how President Trump’s actions are affecting Minnesota and about state GOP plans to reduce fraud and waste.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Trump raises doubts about NATO's mutual defense pact
item.description: President Trump on Thursday suggested the United States may not defend NATO allies that have not contributed enough to defense spending, casting doubt on a core aspect of the alliance.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dem senator behind social media fail responds to Elon Musk's offer
item.description: Sen. Cory Booker said he will not be taking up Elon Musk on an offer after the senator took credit for a Democratic social media mashup against President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The House censures Democratic Rep. Al Green for disrupting Trump's joint address to Congress
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The House on Thursday voted to censure an unrepentant Rep. Al Green, D-Texas, for disrupting President Donald Trump's address to Congress. Green was joined in the well of the House by more than 20 fellow Democrats as Speaker Mike Johnson, R-La., read the censure resolution.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump signs tariff exemptions for certain imports from Canada, Mexico
item.description: President Trump on Thursday signed off on tariff exemptions for imports from Canada and Mexico that are covered under a 2020 North American trade agreement, marking the latest reversal of duties imposed earlier this week. Under the orders, goods compliant with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump prepares order dismantling the Education Department
item.description: The draft executive action, obtained by NPR, acknowledges the department and its signature responsibilities were created by Congress, and cannot legally be altered without congressional approval.
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner, MPR News

item.title: DOGE wants to cut $1 trillion this year. But it’s not looking at big spending drivers
item.description: Even if you take DOGE’s savings claims so far at face value, its moves to cancel contracts, end leases and push federal agencies to reduce head count barely dent the government’s balance sheet.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler, MPR News

item.title: Newsom calls biological men in women's sports 'deeply unfair' in podcast with conservative activist
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom agrees with conservative activist Charlie Kirk that trans women in women's sports is "unfair," just days after a trans athlete in the Golden State took home several wins.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: There are calls to pardon Chauvin. Here’s why it wouldn’t get him out of prison
item.description: Trump can pardon Chauvin’s 20-year federal convictions for violating Floyd’s civil rights, but pardoning or commuting Chauvin’s 22 1/2 year state sentence is out of Trump’s jurisdiction.
item.dc:creator: Anika Besst, MPR News

item.title: Judge declines to immediately spare USAID contractors from firings
item.description: A federal judge on Thursday declined to immediately spare U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) contractors from mass firings, letting move forward a core part of the Trump administration’s effort to dismantle the agency. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House votes to censure Al Green for disrupting Trump speech
item.description: The House voted Thursday to censure Rep. Al Green (D-Texas) for his protest during President Trump’s address to Congress this week — a disruptive display that prompted his removal from the House chamber.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Use of military aircraft for deportations has stopped, source says
item.description: A Defense Department source says the Pentagon has not received a request for military aircraft since Saturday, March 1.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: State of Democra-Z: Three young voters weigh in on Trump’s first weeks in office
item.description: After President Trump’s address to Congress Tuesday night, MPR News host Nina Moini checks in with three college-age voters with differing political views.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini and Alanna Elder, MPR News

item.title: Trump could reduce Mexico, Canada tariffs, commerce secretary says
item.description: "Maybe — he’ll consider giving them relief until we get to, of course, April 2," the Commerce Secretary told Bloomberg.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Wall Street falls again as losses wipe out all post-election gains for the S&P 500
item.description: Stocks tumbled on Wall Street Tuesday as a trade war between the U.S. and its key trading partners escalated and threatened to wipe out all the gains for the S&P 500 since Election Day.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How to watch Trump address to Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump is set to address a joint session of Congress for the first time during his second term on Tuesday, outlining the goals of his administration and how he views the state of the country.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: NYC mayoral candidate Zohran Mamdani in second place in new poll
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – It is under four months away until voters hit the polls for the Democratic primary for New York City mayor. New polling from the Honan Strategy Group showed some interesting results about the race.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Latex glove ban proposed in Nevada to help allergy sufferers
item.description: An allergy to latex can come with a lot of limits on where you can go and what you can do. Some clothing, medical procedures, and even visits to restaurants become potential problems.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News
